body:
https://events.nami.org/namicon2026/home
Hi Brady,
NAMICon 2026 is happening next week! We’re so excited to see you in Atlanta May 27 - May 30 at the Atlanta Marriott Marquis.

To help you maximize your NAMICon experience, here are a few important things you should know before you go.

General Onsite Information

Dates: May 27 – 30, 2026

Location: Atlanta Marriott Marquis (https://www.marriott.com/en-us/hotels/atlmq-atlanta-marriott-marquis/overview/) : 265 Peachtree Center Avenue, Atlanta, GA 30303

NAMICon 2026 Registration  🪪

Registration check-in and the Welcome Desk will be located in the Atrium Foyer, just outside of the Atrium Ballroom, where General Sessions will be held. Badges are required to be worn and are required for entry to all sessions and NAMICon receptions.

Registration Hours:
* Wednesday, May 27 | 8:00 a.m. – 7:00 p.m.
* Thursday, May 28 | 7:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m.
* Friday, May 29 | 8:00 a.m. – 3:00 p.m.

NAMICon Wi-Fi  🛜

Complementary Wi-Fi is available at the Marriott. Feel free to log in using the log in information below.

Network: NAMICon2026

Password: NAMI@ATL

Event Staff Are Available for Support  🫶

We have lanyards and buttons to help identify NAMICon staff and volunteers.
* 💙 Attendee Lanyard (Blue with a white NAMI logo)
* 💚 Event Staff & Volunteers (Solid green)

ADA Support🤝

Seating will be reserved in the front rows of all sessions for those requiring ADA accommodations.  Please visit the Welcome desk with any questions regarding ADA support at the conference.

Translation Services💬

We are utilizing the Wordly Translation App for our mainstage sessions. It aims to enhance event inclusivity by allowing participants to listen or read in real-time in their preferred language. With dozens of language interpretation options available, attendees can access real-time translation and live captioning throughout the experience.

Attendees can access these services onsite by scanning a QR code or entering a URL into their phone browser.

Health and Safety

NAMICon COVID Protocol⛑️

Your safety and the safety of all our attendees, speakers, sponsors, exhibitors, and staff is important to us. NAMI will continue to follow CDC, state, and local guidelines regarding COVID-19 protocols. NAMI reserves the right to change participation protocols depending on COVID-19 conditions, along with local and CDC guidelines in place at the time of the convention.

The guidelines that are in effect on the date of our events will take precedence over our currently planned protocols, which include:
* No vaccine requirements
* Masks are recommended for those who are not vaccinated
* Hand sanitization stations and masks will be readily available

If you are experiencing any flu-like symptoms, please do not travel or attend the event. Send an email to meetings@nami.org (mailto:meetings@nami.org?subject=NAMICon%202026%20Attendance&body=) if you need to cancel your registration due to illness.

Learning and Connecting

Plan Your Agenda with the NAMICon App 📲

Make your NAMICon experience seamless when you download the Swoogo: Go Attend mobile app today! Access the full NAMICon schedule at your fingertips and stay connected with attendees. Available for download in the App Store (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/swoogo-go-attend/id6738286460) and Google Play (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.swoogo.goattend&hl=en_US) .

Continuing Education Credit 📚

Selected sessions in the Science That is Changing Mental Health Care track have been approved for CE Credit. See the attached PDF for more information on these sessions and how to claim credit after the conference. 

NAMICon Celebrations 🎉

We invite you to kick off and close out NAMICon with us in style in the Marquis Ballroom. Enjoy food, drinks, a DJ, and lots of fun with amazing people. We can’t wait to see you there!
* Welcome Reception: Wednesday, May 27 | 6 - 8 p.m.
* Closing Celebration: Friday, May 29 | 6 - 8 p.m.

Quick Tips 💡

Plan Your Experience Ahead
* Customize Your Agenda: With so much on the agenda, you won’t be able to attend everything. So, we highly encourage you to review the program schedule on the NAMICon 2026 app to customize your experience in advance.
* Local Weather:  Please check weather reports accordingly.
* Be Comfortable: There’s a lot to do at NAMICon 2026. Between the sessions, our Exhibit Hall and special events, be sure to take breaks for yourself and wear comfortable shoes! We recommend dressing in layers, as the temperatures in the event spaces may vary.
* Prioritize Your Mental Health: If you need a mental wellness break during NAMICon, we’ve got you covered. Visit the Quiet Space or enjoy a coloring book in one of our lounges to relax and take a breath.
* Get Social: We encourage you to use #NAMICon2026 when posting and sharing your experience across your social media platforms.

Stay Safe and Alert 🚨

It's crucial to prioritize your safety. Stay vigilant and remain aware of your surroundings to ensure a safe experience. When exploring Atlanta, we encourage you to use the buddy system — particularly in the evenings.

Generous support for this information provided by

See You Soon in Atlanta!
Plan Your NAMICon Experience (https://events.nami.org/namicon2026/travel)

#NAMICon2026

Hi Lorraine,

I saw that our concierge reached out after you downloaded the Doro Mind
app, so I wanted to check in directly. If you’re exploring support for
yourself or a loved one, we’re here to help you understand your options and
answer any questions.

If you’re open to it, I’d love to hear what prompted you to download the
app and whether there’s anything you’re looking for right now. Even a quick
reply is helpful.

If you prefer talking live, you can schedule a welcome call anytime here:
https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Doro Mind Enrollment Team

If they still have a problem after your DM, ask for the screen they’re on, what they tapped, and what they expected vs what happened (and a screenshot if anything looks wrong).

body:
Hi Tameran,

This is Pao from the Enrollment Team!

Yes, we absolutely can support you in Utah! While our direct clinical
treatment program (Doro Care) is limited to the Greater New York area, we
offer a national program called *Doro Compass* specifically for families
across the country.
What’s Included in Doro Compass:

   -

   Structured support to build stability and reduce setbacks
   -

   Expert guidance on medication management, work with a nurse practitioner
   overseen by Dr. Robert Laitman, including review of therapeutic drug
   monitoring to optimize results and reduce side effects
   -

      Initial 45-minute session with our psychiatric nurse practitioner to
      review current needs and develop a care plan
      -

      Follow-up sessions, typically two in the first month, and one or two
      in the following months as needed
      -

      Note that direct prescribing is not available under this plan
      -

   Consultations with outside providers to coordinate care and make
   recommendations
   -

   Personalized research and care navigation to help you explore treatment
   options
   -

   On-demand messaging with your Doro Guide or nurse practitioner between
   check-ins
   -

   Family education and coaching through weekly groups, online resources
   and individual sessions

Pricing Details:

Doro Compass offers two flexible tiers of monthly support:

   -

   Tier 2 ($2,100/month): All members begin here with the highest level of
   support to help reduce setbacks and build stability.
   -

   Tier 1 ($700/month): As you or your loved one make progress, our
   clinical team will recommend when it's appropriate to move to this
   lower-intensity tier.

Progression from Tier 2 to Tier 1 is based on recovery progress. While many
families remain in Tier 2 for several months, our goal is to support
eventual transition. We offer income-based financial support. To verify
your eligibility, please share your household size and income.
Next Steps:

When you’re ready to move forward, go to our membership portal
<https://go.doromind.com/purchase> (guide here
<https://www.doromind.com/member-portal-guide>). Note that from time to
time we may have waitlist only <http://doromind.com/waitlist> availability
which you will see on the site. Once we initiate your membership we will
then begin the enrollment process to match you with your care team within 3
business days. If you have further questions to discuss, schedule a call
<https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call> anytime.

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Doro Mind Enrollment Team

 https://qr.doromind.com/

body:
Hi Brian,

Thanks for talking with me Friday. Sorry for the late response, I got a distracted as I looked into previous messages we sent you and tried to roll your grant into this response. Since you already have the info about Doro Care, I'm attaching information about the Doro Compass services, so you'll have it as you navigate the this week.

*Doro Compass* - Available Nationwide in the US

*What’s Included in Doro Compass*

---------------------------------

* 

*Structured support to build stability and reduce setbacks*

* 

*Expert guidance on medication management* , work with a nurse practitioner overseen by Dr. Robert Laitman, including review of therapeutic drug monitoring to optimize results and reduce side effects

* 

Initial 45-minute session with our psychiatric nurse practitioner to review current needs and develop a care plan

* 

Follow-up sessions, typically two in the first month, and one or two in the following months as needed

* 

Note that direct prescribing is not available under this plan

* 

*Consultations with outside provider* s to coordinate care and make recommendations

* 

*Personalized research and care navigation* to help you explore treatment options

* 

*On-demand messaging* with your Doro Guide or nurse practitioner between check-ins

* 

*Family education and coaching* through weekly groups, online resources and individual sessions

*Pricing Details*

-----------------

Doro Compass offers two flexible tiers of monthly support:

* 

*Tier 2 ( $2,100 $840/month): All members begin here with the highest level of support to help reduce setbacks and build stability.*

* 

Tier 1 ( $700 $280/month): As you or your loved one make progress, our clinical team will recommend when it's appropriate to move to this lower-intensity tier.

*Next Steps*

------------

When you’re ready to move forward, go to our membership portal ( https://go.doromind.com/purchase ) ( guide here ( https://www.doromind.com/member-portal-guide ) ). Note that from time to time we may have waitlist only ( http://doromind.com/waitlist ) availability which you will see on the site. Once we initiate your membership we will then begin the enrollment process to match you with your care team within 3 business days. If you have further questions to discuss, schedule a call ( https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call ) anytime.

Cheers,

Brady, for the Doro Mind team

*Connect with us on the Doro Mind app!*

( https://apps.apple.com/us/app/doro-mind/id6670178468 ) ( https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.doromind.app )

body:
CS - 05/19/2026
Coach - Mala

8am C woke up and was dressed ready to leave.

9.15am follow up appointment for leg. Everything looks good and she was
told she would be able to run again.

On the drive back she started worrying about her nose being “too big and
wanting plastic surgery.” I told her it looked fine and was balanced with
her features but she kept going on about how picking it in the past made it
bigger. This is a recurring paranoid thought.  When we got back she
realized she was missing one of her earrings. I told her to check her room,
her bed or on the floor, but she was convinced she lost it at the doctor’s
office.

She called the office and put in a lost inquiry before even checking her
room. A few minutes later she found the earring under her bedsheet. She
will typically jump to conclusions before fully checking things through.
(she also found her house key she lost in her office)

11-12pm  We did an online workout class since there wasn’t time for yoga.

Afterwards she started making breakfast and getting ready. I reminded her a
few times to be ready to leave by 1-1.15pm.  I remembered her saying the
dentist appointment was at 1.30pm. She kept insisting it was at 2pm. Took a
long time getting dressed up changing outfits multiple times.

1pm Meds.

I told her I would wait in the car to hurry her up. Came out, went back
inside because she forgot to brush her teeth. Drove off.  Then wanted to go
back again, she forgot to put her braces back in.  Arrived at the dentist
around 1.50pm. I dropped her off.  10 minutes later she called saying the
appointment was actually at 1.30pm and the receptionist gave the spot to
someone else because she was late. C said the receptionist was really mad
at her. Called her mum upset and asked her to put another date in her
calendar to reminder.  Apparently she put the wrong time on her phone.  Has
a hard time scheduling appointments and writing them into her calendar on
her phone vs writing it to her planner.  Told me last time she went to the
dentist with Janelle on the wrong day.

When we got back she took a short nap.

4.30pm Dr. Price appointment.

5.30pm C tried to book a 7.15pm yoga  But the  waitlist was full. So she
read, made dinner, and organized her packages.

8.30pm meds.

9pm she came into my room asking me to help check the window in her bedroom
from the backyard. because she was worried someone could get in.  She
locked the sliding door but forgot the front door. Prompted her to lock the
front door.

9.30pm went to bed

 ** Supplements - She's not taking them every day.  She took a probiotic
last night and the morning and reported it helped her bowel movement.  She
has an appointment with Merge Medical this thursday.
 
 ----
Secured by Paubox - HITRUST certified
https://www.paubox.com
----

body:
Hi Martha,

Thank you for reaching out to learn more about Doro Mind. Based on your
location and needs you would be the best fit for our Doro Compass program.
I’m writing to share more details about what’s included and help answer any
questions you might have before moving forward.

What’s Included in Doro Compass:

   -

   Structured support to build stability and reduce setbacks
   -

   Expert guidance on medication management, work with a nurse practitioner
   overseen by Dr. Robert Laitman, including review of therapeutic drug
   monitoring to optimize results and reduce side effects
   -

      Initial 45-minute session with our psychiatric nurse practitioner to
      review current needs and develop a care plan
      -

      Follow-up sessions, typically two in the first month, and one or two
      in the following months as needed
      -

      Note that direct prescribing is not available under this plan
      -

   Consultations with outside providers to coordinate care and make
   recommendations
   -

   Personalized research and care navigation to help you explore treatment
   options
   -

   On-demand messaging with your Doro Guide or nurse practitioner between
   check-ins
   -

   Family education and coaching through weekly groups, online resources
   and individual sessions

Pricing Details:

Doro Compass offers two flexible tiers of monthly support:

   -

   Tier 2 ($2,100/month $840/mo with financial support): All members begin
   here with the highest level of support to help reduce setbacks and build
   stability.
   -

   Tier 1 ($700/month $280/mo with financial support): As you or your loved
   one make progress, our clinical team will recommend when it's appropriate
   to move to this lower-intensity tier with fewer touchpoints but continued
   guidance.

Progression from Tier 2 to Tier 1 is based on recovery progress. While many
families remain in Tier 2 for several months, our goal is to support
eventual transition. We offer income-based financial support. To verify
your eligibility, please share your household size and income.
Next Steps:

When you’re ready to move forward, go to our membership portal
<https://go.doromind.com/purchase> (guide here
<https://www.doromind.com/member-portal-guide>). Note that from time to
time we may have waitlist only <http://doromind.com/waitlist> availability
which you will see on the site. Once we initiate your membership we will
then begin the enrollment process to match you with your care team within 3
business days. If you have further questions to discuss, schedule a call
<https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call> anytime.
On the other hand, I can confirm your welcome call booked with Brady on
Friday, May 22nd at 1 PM ET.

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Doro Mind Enrollment Team

 https://qr.doromind.com/

Subject: Re: Appointment for my son

Hi Tameran,

Thank you for reaching out. It sounds like we could be a good fit to support you and your son. We organize our services in monthly memberships and do not offer individual appointments. I have attached a summary of our Doro Care services. Please note that this assumes that you live in NY. We can only provide direct care in NY currently, however, we do have team based consulting services in other states which can help with medication optimization.

If you are ready to move forward please complete our request form<https://form.jotform.com/242495198869175> and purchase a spot on our waitlist<https://go.doromind.com/purchase> to be in the queue for enrollment. If you have further questions to discuss, please schedule a call<https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call> anytime.

~Stephanie

body:
Hi Jessica,

My name is Brittany Troupe, and I will be your Lead Guide at Doro Mind. I
am here to support you by answering questions, providing resources,
facilitating communication with the rest of the Doro team, and supporting
your skills as a family. I'm looking forward to meeting you and Payton and
supporting your journey with us. This email details a few next steps for
starting this journey. We wanted to get your family started as quickly as
possible, and to be fully transparent, we have a few scheduling limits for
the next few weeks. This means my colleague Kendra, copied here, will also
help support your next steps.

*Below are some next steps to get started: *

   - Please complete your Intake Form <https://forms.gle/UP9wtCR9o4bWLjED9>.
   Completing this helps us coordinate care within our team and ensures you
   have access to high quality support from the start.
      - If you have any additional information in other documents—such as a
      written narrative of Payton's history, neuropsychological exams,
recent lab
      reports, etc.—please email them to guides@doromind.com.

   - The second step will be to schedule an Introduction to Doro Mind
   session with a Guide to help you get started and know what to expect with
   Doro Mind over the next few months. This week and next, I am going to be
   out of the office for blocks of time, so my team member, Kendra Wilson,
   will be the one to meet with you to cover this initial session. Kendra
   is available this week on Thursday the 21st between 2:00-4:00pm Eastern
   Time, or Friday the 22nd between 12:00-3:00 Eastern. Please let us know
   what day and time works best for a one hour Intro meeting.

   - During your intro call, Kendra will review information provided so
      far, go through the Doro Mind app and service, and explore your initial
      questions and goals for your first meeting with Clare, your Nurse
      Practitioner, and schedule that visit. Clare is available next week on
      Tuesday the 26th between 12:00-3:00 Eastern. Would you be
available during
      that window of time for an inital virtual meeting with Clare?

Please let me know if you have any questions or need any support prior to
your first appointment. We look forward to getting to know you.

~Brittany

-- 
Brittany Troupe. NBC-HWC
Doro Guide

*Doro Mind Inc. <https://www.doromind.com/>*

*Rebuild & Find Hope*
summary: Brittany onboards Jessica/Payton with intake form, intro session options, NP scheduling.
potential_work_reason: member onboarding email not addressed to me

body:
*Product Backlog v6: changes from today's huddle*

Made the following structural changes to the <https://app.asana.com/1/1205801040312777/project/1214760215678681/list|Product Backlog> before next Tuesday:

• Added an *Inbox* column before *Align* for uncommitted ideas (originally floated as "Parking Lot").
• Removed the *Status RYG* column. Status is now represented inline in each milestone title via the :large_green_circle:/:large_yellow_circle:/:red_circle: prefix.
• Added *RACI custom fields* on every milestone: Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed (all people-pickers, pinned to the top of each card).
• Backfilled *Responsible + Accountable* on all assigned milestones (current Assignee copied to both). Consulted + Informed left empty for now.

4 milestones are still unassigned (no R/A populated): *Recovery planning*, *Vitals & Labs data capture*, *On-Call Support (provider-side)*, *Lab Cadence Tracker 2.0*. Owners welcome to claim or nominate.

*RACI definitions*

> Note: *DRI maps to Accountable* in this model. We're using RACI instead of a standalone DRI field because R + A together cover the same concept with sharper nuance.
• *Responsible (R):* the person doing the work. Can be more than one, but keep it to one or two ideally.
• *Accountable (A):* the person who owns the outcome and signs off "done." Exactly one person. This is the DRI.
• *Consulted (C):* people whose input shapes the work (two-way comms). SMEs and stakeholders whose buy-in you need before shipping.
• *Informed (I):* people who need to know the outcome but aren't part of the decision (one-way comms).

*Before next Tuesday's huddle:*
• Each owner: review your milestone's R/A and fill in C/I where applicable.
• Unassigned milestones: claim or nominate ownership.
• Tuesday's huddle will open with backlog review instead of verbal updates per person.
summary: David Chang: Product Backlog v6 structural changes summary.
potential_work_reason: FYI channel broadcast, no @mention of me

body:
Hi Dr. Laitman,

Skyler reported having a rough day. She is grieving the loss of her long
time cat, Avi. There is no plan to act on these urges, but I wanted you to
be advised of the passive suicidal ideations. Please let me know if you
have any suggestions as I continue to work with her.

Skyler's words are in green.

So far it’s a little hard to accept living right now, I want to be with my
cat, my sweet Avi girl

Clinical Review · NP

Flagged by Thedo Robot
Mark completeDelete flag

I’m really glad you told me. I can hear how much pain and longing you’re
carrying right now, especially missing Avi. When things feel this hard, I
want to check in gently with you. Are you having any thoughts of acting on
not living? Or are these more thoughts about wanting the pain and heaviness
to stop?
12:38 PM
[image: Ama W.]
Ama W.12:41 PM

More so of thoughts of wanting the pain to stop but also not wanting to
live so that I can be with my Avi

Urgent Review · NP

Flagged by Thedo Robot
Mark completeDelete flag

That makes a lot of sense to me...it sounds less like you want your life to
end, and more like you’re hurting so deeply and missing Avi so intensely
that being alive feels very hard right now. Did I get that right? I want to
check in clearly though because YOUR safety matters to me. Are you having
any urges to act on these thoughts?
12:44 PM
[image: Ama W.]
Ama W.12:49 PM

Yeah that makes sense. I don’t have an actual plan to act on these feelings
but really just the want and desire. I have ECT this week so that should
help a little bit

-- 
Light & Lava,
Doro Guide
Heather Anderson
summary: Heather flags Skyler's passive suicidal ideation to Dr Laitman after grief over cat.
potential_work_reason: clinical notice to Dr Laitman; Brady CC observer

body:
Hi Martha,

This is Pao from the Enrollment Team.

Thank you for reaching out to us. We received your chat and would like to
offer our support to you and your family. At Doro Mind, we specialize in
helping families and individuals navigating psychosis, particularly through
the expert use of clozapine and comprehensive side-effect management.

We offer two programs:

   - Doro Care (available in NY, NJ, CT): direct psychiatric and
   therapeutic treatment
   - Doro Compass (available nationwide): expert guidance and coaching for
   families, medication support, and collaboration with outside providers.

To help us better understand your specific situation and provide tailored
recommendations, we invite you to complete our short intake form
<https://go.doromind.com/referral>.

If you would like to speak with our team directly, schedule a welcome call
<https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call>.

We look forward to connecting and helping you navigate the next steps
toward recovery.

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Doro Mind Enrollment Team

 https://qr.doromind.com/

body:
Hi Debbie,

Thank you for taking the time to share your information with us.

After a careful review of your eligibility form and clinical requirements,
it appears that our program isn't the right fit for your specific needs at
this time.

Please know that our eligibility criteria are designed to ensure the safety
and success of every participant, and this decision is based on our current
clinical guidelines.

We know this may not be the news you were hoping for, and we sincerely
appreciate the time you invested in applying. Thank you again for
connecting with us, and we wish you all the best moving forward.

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Doro Mind Enrollment Team

sent last week.

lot to take on. Whether you prefer to share details via our *intake form

body: Email from NAMI Queens/Nassau Don't Miss Out! Register Now to Access Vital Resources and Community Support. In this eNews Educational Meeting Woman's Mental Health Month Spotlight on Support: New Family Support Group HYBRID Co-Ocurring Support Group ﻿NAMI Home Front Support Groups TEN different support groups﻿ ﻿ Unites Socialization Groups﻿ ﻿ ﻿Community Updates TD Awareness Educational Meeting: ﻿Woman's Mental Health Month WOMEN’S HEALTH MEETING A Conversation on Women’s Wellness, Mental Health & Support June 17, 2026 Time & Meeting Details Coming Soon Join us for an open and supportive discussion centered around women’s health and wellness. Together, we’ll create space for conversation, connection, education, and support surrounding topics that impact women’s mental and physical well-being. Whether you are looking to learn, share, listen, or simply feel connected, all are welcome to attend this meaningful virtual gathering. Please save the date and stay tuned for additional details and registration information. ﻿Spotlight on Support Co-Occurring Disorders Now Offered In-Person & on Zoom Co-Occurring Disorders Support Group For Caregivers Led by David Hymowitz Time: 7:00 PM – 8:30 PM Date: The second Thursday of each month Location: UUC Shelter Rock RE Room 19 (please keep left as you enter campus) Now offered in-person with a virtual (Zoom) option available This group is designed for caregivers supporting a loved one living with both a mental health condition and a substance use disorder (co-occurring disorder). Together, we will explore how these conditions interact and share practical, supportive strategies to better understand and care for your loved one. This is a welcoming space to connect with others who understand what you are experiencing. This group is free and open to all. Please register in advance to attend and to receive the Zoom link. Click here to register We look forward to welcoming you, whether in person or on Zoom. NAMIQN Family Support Group Caring for a loved one who is struggling can feel overwhelming and isolating, but you don’t have to go through it by yourself. The NAMIQN Family Support Group lead by David and Gail Sills is a welcoming, peer-led space for adults supporting someone experiencing symptoms of a mental health condition. Thursdays 6:30 PM to 8:00 PM Virtually What You’ll Gain: A safe place to talk openly and be heard Connection with people who truly understand what you’re going through Support, encouragement, and real-life insight from others on a similar path In this supportive and confidential space, you can share your experiences, listen without judgment, and begin to feel a sense of hope. Together, we create a community built on empathy, understanding, and connection. You’ll walk away feeling supported, empowered, and better equipped to navigate your journey. Click here to register NOTE: Participants will remain anonymous to the VA or any of the service branches. Click to register and learn more NAMI Homefront Education Program A free, 6-session educational program for families, caregivers, and friends of military service members and veterans living with mental health conditions. Based on NAMI’s nationally recognized Family-to-Family program, NAMI Homefront is tailored to the unique experiences of those who serve — and those who support them. Classes are led by trained family members who understand this journey firsthand. What You’ll Gain: Tools to manage stress and care for yourself Ways to support your loved one with understanding and compassion Guidance on navigating services and resources Education on mental health conditions, treatments, and recovery Skills for communication, problem-solving, and crisis support Hope. Support. Practical tools. Hope. Support. Practical tools. NAMIQN: Unites Unites Socializations Groups Connecting, Socializing, Thriving Unites Monday Nights, 6:30 – 8:30PM, Sloman Auditorium and Virtual NAMI Queens/Nassau is thrilled to feature Unites by NAMI, a groundbreaking socialization group offering adults living with mental health challenges a supportive space to connect, bond, and have fun. Weekly Activities Include: Game Night – Challenge yourself with new games or enjoy familiar favorites. A perfect way to meet others with similar interests! Karaoke Night – Share your favorite songs solo or as a group! Discover new music, enjoy performances, and have a great time. Art & Creative Writing – Express yourself through art or storytelling, with opportunities to share and connect. Gentle Yoga – Relax, recharge, and focus on wellness in a supportive environment. Knitting & More – Craft, create, and bond with peers over shared interests. Know someone who’s struggling? Invite them to join Unites by NAMI! Together, we can build a supportive and welcoming community. Click here to register! REGISTER FOR UNITES TODAY Support Groups: NAMI Support is here for you both virtually and in person when noted! Black and Brown Wellness Caregivers Support Group (Virtual) This group is currently on pause. we will let you know as soon as it is up and running again. Group Leaders: Jennifer (taylorwins24@gmail.com) & Carmine Roche (carmine.roche@gmail.com) ﻿Sharing and Caring Support Group - Virtual: Every Sunday from 7:30-8:30 PM Register once and use the same Zoom link each week. If you are new, a Zoom link will be emailed to you before the group. Group Leaders: Lee Israel, lisrael@namiqn.org, Gail Keren, gailkeren57@gmail.com Family Support Group - Virtual: Tuesdays 6:30-8:00 Group Leaders: David and Gail Sills dsills@namiqn.org Peer Support Group: Tuesdays 2:00-3:00 PM Group Leaders: Jessica Garfinkel and Kimberly Uckele, jgarfinkel628@gmail.com Peer Support Group - Virtual: Thursdays 6:30-7:30 PM Group Leaders: Katie and Toni Ann jgarfinkel628@gmail.com Co-Occurring Support Group: Caregivers - Join us in person or virtually (register to receive the Zoom link) Thursdays 7:00-8:30 PM - 1/8 2/12, 3/12, 4/9, 5/14, 6/11 7/9, 8/13, 9/10, 10/8, 11/12, 12/10  Group Leader: David Hymowitz, office@namiqn.org Family to Family- In Person: Beginning Thursday Feburary 19th 6:30-8:30 PM Group Leaders: Lee Israel, lisrael@namiqn.org, and Maria Ceraulo mceraulo0116@gmail.com  REGISTRATION IS REQUIRED Caregiver Support Group: Mental Health Disorder - In person Thursdays 7:00-8:30 PM – 1/22, 2/26, 3/26, 4/23, 5/28, 6/25, 7/23, 8/27, 9/24, 10/22, 11/19,12/17  Group Leader: David Hymowitz, office@namiqn.org NEW Family Support Group - Virtual: Thursdays 6:30-8:00 Group Leaders: David and Gail Sills dsills@namiqn.org NAMIQN: Connections Mondays 6:30 pm-8:00 pm Group Leaders: Jessica Garfinkel and Kimberly Uckele jgarfinkel628@gmail.com Click to Register for a Support Group! NAMIQN Community Updates FREE Mental Health First Aid Training Older Adult Module Learn how to recognize the signs of mental health and substance use challenges in older adults and how to provide support through this evidence-based certification training. Training Details FCA Main Office 377 Oak Street, 5th Floor Garden City, NY 11530 10:00 AM – 4:30 PM (6.5-hour class with 30-minute break) Upcoming 2026 Dates • April 1 & April 30 • May 8 & May 14 • June 15 Contact & Registration: Jeanne Cacciatore jcacciatore@fcali.org Call/Text: (516) 619-6786 Office: (516) 485-3425 x2332 🌐 www.mentalhealthfirstaid.org Presented by Family & Children’s Association (FCA)  💬 Ask Dr. Honigfeld We’re thrilled to launch Ask Dr. Gil Honigfeld, the renowned “Godfather of Clozapine.” He’s ready to answer your big-picture questions about clozapine’s history, breakthroughs, and its role in treating serious mental illness. While he can’t offer personal medical advice, he can share his unmatched expertise. Have a question you’ve always wondered about? Submit your question here! Answers will be posted on our blog. Don’t miss this unique opportunity! Tardive Dyskinesia Awareness Become a Member of NAMI Queens/Nassau! Join our community of advocates, families, and individuals dedicated to improving lives affected by mental health challenges. As a member, you’ll gain access to resources, events, and a powerful network of support — while helping us expand our impact across Queens and Nassau. Click here to become a member!!   View as Webpage NAMI Queens/Nassau | P.O. Box 176 | Carle Place, NY 11514 US Unsubscribe | Update Profile | Constant Contact Data Notice

body:
Hi Melissa,

This is Pao from the Enrollment Team.

Thank you for reaching out to us. We received your email inquiry and would
like to offer our support to you and your family. At Doro Mind, we
specialize in helping families and individuals navigating psychosis,
particularly through the expert use of clozapine and comprehensive
side-effect management.

We offer two programs:

   - Doro Care (available in NY, NJ, CT): direct psychiatric and
   therapeutic treatment
   - Doro Compass (available nationwide): expert guidance and coaching for
   families, medication support, and collaboration with outside providers.

To help us better understand your specific situation and provide tailored
recommendations, we invite you to complete our short intake form
<https://go.doromind.com/referral>.

If you would like to speak with our team directly, schedule a welcome call
<https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call>.

On the other hand, to answer your questions, we do not have physical
offices in Staten Island; our in-person care evaluations take place in Mt.
Kisco or Williamsburg, Brooklyn. Afterward, virtual visits are available.
We do not accept insurance directly but should be able to accept HSA cards
and can provide superbills for out-of-network reimbursement.

Regarding bloodwork, safety guidelines and draw frequencies are still
closely tied to clinical oversight. We can’t give you a personalized answer
to how frequently blood draws are needed until you have your evaluation,
however, typically blood draws become much less frequent after the first 2
years of successful clozapine treatment. Our medical team evaluates
schedules individually to ensure your routine remains both safe and
effective.

We look forward to connecting and helping you navigate the next steps
toward recovery.

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Doro Mind Enrollment Team

 https://qr.doromind.com/
summary: Pao introduces Doro Care/Compass to Melissa and answers Staten Island and bloodwork questions.

body:
Hi Charlene,

I completely hear you. Navigating life right after a four-month hospital
stay while managing SSI paperwork is incredibly draining, and it is
completely normal to feel emotionally spent and overwhelmed. Protecting his
current stability is your top priority, and we don't want to rush you into
any changes before you are ready.

To help ease some of your concerns:

   -

   The Tiers & Cost: Both tiers are designed to support recovery through
   guided medication management, coaching, and consultation with outside
   providers. The primary difference is the intensity and frequency of
   services. Unfortunately, it is not as simple as choosing which tier you
   prefer; rather, our clinical team determines the level of care required.
   All families must start at Tier 2 to build that essential stability before
   the clinical team assesses when it's time to step down to Tier 1.
   -

   The Oral Medication & Anosognosia: Lack of insight (anosognosia) is very
   common. Clozapine has unique benefits for treatment resistance and overall
   trajectory. Our approach relies on a very slow titration protocol (which
   typically means starting alongside other meds and then transitioning). We
   also practice highly assertive side-effect management, which drastically
   improves tolerability and helps young adults stay compliant without feeling
   overwhelmed by side effects.

Take all the time you need to settle into his outpatient routine. When you
feel ready to speak with us and explore if this could be a fit for the
future, you are very welcome to find a time for a phone call here:
https://go.doromind.com/welcome-call.

We are here whenever you are ready.

Best Regards,

Pao Rizo

Doro Mind Enrollment Team

 https://qr.doromind.com/

Deep gets meeting notes and action items automatically with Circleback.

Accept 30 free days from Deep https://mt-link.circleback.ai/cl/jgSmPV0NovOYUApiw_mmduE4hsK9SVty09Q3zoU_ft.6o4.eJLqGEpjqfGpa9kWxf0VkuHmqyrFEM6RZFXOo7hrVp0DFuckKSMaqrCgSLRJucOWtN_kuGvxvlzySsH8f8b28B3AxMpIve5YQwZg01cHYgUOeJy1pky00XGzHKAm67KjWPIRZzOc9RY4tBDQ_MAyNJO2Az8PVKggVSUBLFR9OF_T7mdiuAD1.DsxPvlLVcEqDglFpcpZYO04ytxrDtdMVqxbOn4wd0vO7.0ZLqrDLz.uA_oXxc.bAbZqd2D3MQNw0y7S4jbYWOOWNlZPQ1u3cg_1V7qOBR7vTWVCO_J0.mVQN0.6Fz1E6bw.HvIRMUjoVVrKNzSbn6bVMtBhWhExfpyw_1m6c08m3WZewG4odwSXXncGc0VBI--Lh1bfZoGCsSuRB_k--c_2W0xGVWIG_Y9gVCsNpAg~~

ACTION ITEMS

Anisha KalyaniCoordinate a session with Sequoia's healthcare consulting team for Amera

 

Louise and Deep flagged this as the most useful next step for Amera — connecting with Sequoia's healthcare consulting team.

EWEmily WandererIntro Mimi to Sequoia's healthcare consulting team

 

Mimi wants to understand what Sequoia's healthcare consulting team is seeing on the complex behavioral health side, especially around employer plan gaps.

EWEmily WandererSchedule a deep-dive call with Santosh on Rovi Health

 

Santosh wants to get in front of Emily and Jed to walk through Rovi Health's solution and explore a potential partnership.

OVERVIEW

 * Sequoia's Emily Wanderer (Director of Partnerships) and Jed Cloern (VP Business Consulting) walked the SemperVirens accelerator cohort through how to engage with Sequoia as a distribution partner — covering partnership tiers, vetting, GTM, and realistic timelines
 * Sequoia is highly selective and narrow in focus: investor-backed, people-first tech companies, primarily in California and New York — harder to get in, but the flywheel is strong once you're in
 * Last year was the hardest renewal season Jed has seen in 22 years — clients spending $15K–$20K per person annually are seeing double-digit renewals, with biosimilars the only meaningful cost-containment tailwind
 * Formal partnership process takes several months and includes security/compliance review, RFI, and executive sign-off — founders at seed stage should start the process now and aim to get one or two clients on board to prove out the product before broader rollout
 * Three next steps identified: intro to Sequoia's healthcare consulting team (Amera and Doro Mind), and a dedicated deep-dive session for Rovi Health with Emily and Jed

SEQUOIA OVERVIEW & CLIENT SEGMENTS

 * Sequoia focuses on total comp and benefits advisory — compensation, benefits, 401k, and risk — for investor-backed tech companies globally, across 138 countries
 * Three market segments: PEO (roughly 20 to a few hundred employees), mid-market (50–500 domestic, typically fully insured), and enterprise (500+ domestic, typically self-insured or alternative funding)
 * Jed noted companies are moving into alternative funding arrangements earlier than they used to

HEALTHCARE COST ENVIRONMENT

 * Last year was the hardest renewal season Jed has seen in his 22 years — clients already spending $15K–$20K per person per year are facing double-digit renewals
 * At advisory board meetings with United Healthcare and Cigna, biosimilars are the only factor cited as bending the cost curve downward — everything else is moving in the wrong direction
 * 53% of companies with 100–499 employees are taking direct action to reduce healthcare costs, while 32% are expanding mental health access and 26% are expanding family-forming benefits

PARTNERSHIP TIERS & MODELS

 * Three tiers: Discover (referral only, no rev-share, lighter compliance review), Preferred/Priority (referral-based, 15–30% rev-share, rigorous vetting), and Bundle (reseller model, co-selling with pre-negotiated PEPM pricing, Sequoia holds the contract)
 * The bundle is effectively a reseller arrangement — Sequoia handles selling, file exchanges, communications, and client support, which lets vendors capture SMB clients they'd otherwise skip
 * Bundle and Preferred tiers are not necessarily exclusive by category — Spring Health and Headspace both sit in the bundle, and Peloton and Wellhub coexist there too, as long as there's meaningful differentiation
 * Sequoia is heading into a new fiscal year and actively tightening its partnership strategy — the network is becoming more exclusive, not less

PARTNER VETTING PROCESS

 * Vetting criteria covers four areas: mission alignment with Sequoia's high-touch model, market gap or direct client request, operational capacity to support scale, and partnership strength with mutual clients or proven outcomes
 * Security and compliance requirements include SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA, role-based access control, and SSO — Sequoia looks for detailed policies, resolution plans with timelines, and historical proof of improvement
 * The full process — RFI, multi-team review, executive sign-off — typically takes several months
 * Jed's preference is to recommend things that are already somewhat vetted in the market — it's easier to champion a vendor a client has already adopted than to be the first to stick your neck out on something brand new

GTM & CO-MARKETING WITH SEQUOIA

 * Sequoia is place-based and community-oriented — intimate dinners, office coffee carts and breakfasts for GTM teams, and in-person event sponsorships work best for building awareness
 * Transform (annual conference, historically in March) is a key venue — this year introduced "FastPass," where sponsors buy guaranteed 15-minute first meetings with decision-makers at target companies
 * Some SemperVirens portfolio companies have ended up as tiles in the Sequoia mobile app, giving clients and employees direct visibility into available services
 * Webinars and co-authored content are also available but are lower on the impact spectrum compared to in-person activations

CARROT FERTILITY GROWTH STORY

 * Carrot is the clearest example of a SemperVirens portfolio company scaling through Sequoia — SemperVirens invested at seed (2016–17), Carrot was added to the bundle at 50 employees, and now has 1M covered lives and 110+ bundle clients
 * The Roe v. Wade overturn in 2022 was a significant external accelerator, but the bundle distribution was already in place to capture that demand
 * Emily's point: the bundle helped Carrot reach SMB clients at scale without Carrot having to sell them directly

GETTING STARTED AS A PARTNER

 * Emily suggested the right crawl-walk-run approach: go through the formal process, get one or two early adopter clients on board, apply their feedback to the product, then scale — consistent with how Carrot and One Medical both started
 * Jed noted that upmarket enterprise clients are sometimes the ones who push new solutions to Sequoia first — either because they want innovative cost containment or because they're competing for talent against companies like Google and Apple
 * The bundle also gives enterprise clients a low-friction way to test vendors — OpenAI used it to trial vendors and then moved to direct contracts once value was proven

TPA-FACING PRODUCTS & HEALTHCARE CONSULTING TEAM

 * Jed confirmed Sequoia's healthcare consulting team does make TPA and stop-loss recommendations — captives like Pareto require a TPA (e.g., Meritain, UMR, Collective), and the enterprise team has a dedicated person for those decisions
 * PBM recommendations are also part of that team's scope — Rightway was cited as an example they've worked with
 * The healthcare consulting team is a separate channel from the wellbeing partnerships team, so TPA-adjacent products would route differently than point solutions

BEHAVIORAL HEALTH GAPS IN EMPLOYER PLANS

 * Mimi raised that health plans have contracted heavily for lower-acuity behavioral health but left significant gaps on the complex/serious mental illness side — she's seeing parallel interest from health plans in filling those gaps
 * Jed acknowledged he's not the right person to speak to this and offered to connect Doro Mind with Sequoia's healthcare consulting team for a more informed conversation

body:
Hi Arielle,

Here is a summary (with med plan) of my visit with Eli today.

Intermittent Suicidal Ideation (SI): SI has decreased in frequency with no
current thoughts of self-harm. Safety Plan & Check-ins: Eli advised to
track depression and SI daily. A safety protocol was discussed for high SI,
which includes wellness checks if he becomes unresponsive. Family and the
support system remain involved.

Clozapine: Increased to 25 mg nightly (up from 12.5 mg) via slow titration
to minimize side effects. Lithium Extended-Release: Initiated at a low dose
of 300 mg nightly. Planned increase to 450 mg after one week. Next dose of
the Invega LAI will be decreased to 156 mg. Eli is on board with this plan.

BP & HR Monitoring: Eli must perform twice-weekly blood pressure and heart
rate monitoring (both sitting and standing) to check for orthostatic
hypotension related to Clozapine.

Upcoming Lab Orders: TSH, T4, A1c, BMP, prolactin, HSCRP, and Vitamin B12.
Given Eli's sensitivity to perceived side effects, objective data will help
reassure him.

I know you are meeting with him tomorrow. Let me know if you have any
concerns.

thanks so much,
Clare

body:
CS - 5/18/2026

Coach - Mala

9am C woke up.

9.30-11.30. Yoga

C and her mum face timed with her 2 year old nephew before class.

12.30pm left to go to Lowe’s to buy a safe. She already has one but can’t
find the key. I reminded her there’s also a code and asked if she
remembered it. She said yes but still  needed another one.

After driving off, she asked to turn back home because she forgot her
phone. I pointed out her phone was in her hand. She then admitted it was
actually for something else  and she didn’t want to say. I told her she
didn’t need to tell me but to just say that instead of making things up.

1.30 pm  meds.

Clozapine now at 250mg and holding at this dose.

Metformin 2,000mg taken with 1pm meds.

2-4pm  returned clothes to stores and mailed some back at the post office.
She claimed to be nauseous, but said it was from the anticipation of
returning her sneakers to the store.  Said it was embarrassing. We stopped
to get oyster crackers and ginger ale which seemed to help.

She became very focused on her Golden Goose sneakers and decided they were
now too small and wanted to return them. She had cleaned them and packed
them back into the box. She had originally exchanged them for this size and
had customized the style and colours. Last week she told me the issue
wasn’t the size but that she regretted the colours she customized and felt
they didn’t match other things she wanted to wear.

She then wanted to buy another pair she saw and put these on consignment
instead. Called her mum to discuss since the sneakers are over $600. Her
mum told her she’s been shopping too much and needs to scale back. C argued
back that she had already returned over $600 worth of clothes and said she
feels like she won’t make friends unless she has the right name brand/style
of clothing and shoes.

After hanging up she called her mum back  preoccupied with a paranoid
thought about a girl named Lola back in NYC. She worried Lola may have
replaced her rings and jewelry with fake ones.  C stayed stuck on this
thought for a while, but her mum was eventually able to reassure her it
wouldn’t be possible since she hasn’t seen Lola in many years.

I also redirected by asking if we needed to stop at Whole Foods and had C
make a list of what we needed for dinner to shift her focus.

5-6pm read her book and set an alarm.

6-7pm made dinner together. She showed me some THC gummies in a container
and asked if she should try them. I asked where she got them from but she
wouldn’t say, only telling me Dr. Price said it was okay to take. I told
her I didn’t think it was a good idea with her current meds and suggested
she ask her about them at  tomorrow's appointment. I added it would
probably make her extremely hungry. She left them on the table and went on
a walk alone.

She then asked for help returning something on Amazon that was past the
return date. I talked her through the process and helped her call customer
service.

8.30pm meds.

Klonopin reduced to 0.25mg.

Later she became very preoccupied with sending Eric a thank you text for
the invite. Kept trying to get my attention. Even though I kept telling her
I was on the phone and to give me 5 mins. She  called her mum for help
writing it and then asked me to look over it before sending. After sending
it she immediately second guessed herself and worried she had said the
wrong thing. I reassured her the message sounded perfectly fine, but she
continued waiting for a response and checking for one. I told her he could
simply be busy and not have his phone close by. And not to sit around
waiting for a reply.

She went to her room and closed the door but left the lights on. About 15
minutes later she came out excited because he had replied. After that she
appeared more settled, went back to her room, and turned the lights off.
 
 ----
Secured by Paubox - HITRUST certified
https://www.paubox.com
----
summary: Mala's coach summary for C on 5/18: yoga, sneaker returns, paranoid thought, meds, THC gummies question.

body:
﻿Hello Pao,

  

Yes I did have a chance to look over the information you sent. This is a wonderful program you have and we would love to work with you however two things are giving me pause:

  

1\. The cost. We cannot afford the Tier 1 phase.

  

2\. I am concerned about the form of Clozapine. My son has not done well with oral medication thus far and although he’s compliant at the moment with his monthly shot plus daily oral dosages morning and evening he’s already complaining about taking the oral dosages and is blaming them for the side effects he’s experiencing whereas he’s fine with taking the shot monthly. I’m concerned he wouldn’t be successful on an oral only medication at his stage of life. He’s young, still has a measure of anosognosia so I’m wondering if that will reduce his chances of success. 

  

We have recently been through the wringer and don’t want to make any changes that might destabilize him right now. I’m certainly open to learning more but am overwhelmed with information at this moment and emotionally spent. He just got out of a 4 month hospital stay and we are busy getting him set up with his outpatient care and applying for SSI. So I’m a bit overwhelmed at the moment.

  

Charlene Witek

  

Sent from my iPhone

  
[chain history truncated]

body:
I think you might have something but I would bring in other researchers especially Heidi Taipale and D Kelly. Best Rob
Sent from my iPhone

> On May 16, 2026, at 4:20 PM, Mimi Liu <mimi@doromind.com> wrote:
> 
> ﻿
> Hi Rob and Ann,
> 
> With the raise behind us, Brady and I have been focusing on growth. We see potential in building awareness of Doro Mind through data-driven insights. Along those lines, Eric and I have been kicking around an idea for an academic project, and I wanted to float it before we go further.
> 
> Background: the existing clozapine cost effectiveness analysis (CEA) literature is rich but takes the "treatment-resistant" designation as a given. What's never been formally modeled is the harm of the TR-discovery process itself. Patients spend months-to-years on suboptimal antipsychotics before earning the label and getting escalated to clozapine, and the clinical literature is clear that this delay matters (Yoshimura's 2.8-year response window, Hatano on rehospitalization, Bhavsar on violence, Taipale on mortality). The new Li 2026 JAMA Psychiatry RCT supports starting clozapine after just one failed antipsychotic in first-episode psychosis. Eric ran a deep evidence search and confirmed the formal CEA hasn't been done.
> 
> Eric's a trauma surgeon at Lahey but he's a CEA methodologist by training and runs these models himself in TreeAge. His proposal: build a CEA comparing early clozapine vs the standard TR-discovery pathway in first-episode psychosis, anchored on Li 2026. Target deliverable would be a peer-reviewed paper (JAMA Psych or similar) plus a companion translation piece for the policymaker/payor audience.
> 
> A framing analogy we're playing with for the discussion section: imagine an oncologist saying "let's start with the gentlest chemo first because real treatment has scary side effects, and if your tumors grow over the next year we'll escalate." That's malpractice in oncology. The CEA argues it's effectively the same logic driving current first-line antipsychotic prescribing in early psychosis.
> 
> Team we're sketching: Eric as first author and analyst; Rob in a middle-author role for clinical context (real prescribing pathway, prescriber-anxiety dynamics, what TR-discovery looks like in practice); Deanna Kelly as senior author candidate to anchor academic credibility. I'd be on acknowledgments with full Doro COI disclosure rather than the author line; cleaner reviewer optics that way.
> 
> Would love your reactions. If we want to move forward, the natural next step is a kickoff call to talk through model scope.
> 
> Mimi
summary: Rob Laitman responds to CEA pitch suggesting Heidi Taipale and D Kelly as researchers
potential_work_reason: FYI reply with suggestion, not an ask directed at me
